title: AMD HLS and implementation interpretation rules for the supported 2024.2 surface

metadata.content_policy: Project-authored short paraphrases and citations only; referenced documents are not redistributed.
metadata.coverage_boundary: AMD Vitis HLS and Vivado 2024.2, one kernel/component, current public extractors
metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Stable hashes and snapshot membership, fail-closed evidence closure, mapping ambiguity handling, and correctness/resource/timing gate construction are HLSGraph policies. Vendor citations establish only the source semantics stated by each rule.

documents.title: Vitis High-Level Synthesis User Guide
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the guide is not included in HLSGraph.

documents.title: Vivado Design Suite User Guide: Using Constraints
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the guide is not included in HLSGraph.

documents.title: Vivado Design Suite Tcl Command Reference Guide
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the guide is not included in HLSGraph.

documents.title: Vivado Design Suite User Guide: Design Analysis and Closure Techniques
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the guide is not included in HLSGraph.

documents.title: Vivado Design Suite User Guide: Power Analysis and Optimization
documents.license_note: Reference metadata only; the guide is not included in HLSGraph.

rules.title: Keep requested directives separate from achieved behavior
rules.summary: An optimization request can be relaxed or unmet, so its declared, tool-effective, and achieved states must remain separate.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Bind DATAFLOW to the region it transforms
rules.summary: DATAFLOW applies to a function or loop region and asks the tool to overlap eligible tasks through channels; the pragma alone does not prove the resulting topology.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Treat pipeline II as a request until scheduling
rules.summary: PIPELINE requests concurrent iterations or calls, but dependencies and resources can force an achieved II above the requested value.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Scope UNROLL and distinguish its factor from achieved structure
rules.summary: UNROLL requests concurrent loop iterations and can replicate operations; its factor is a declaration until the transformed schedule is observed.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Keep ARRAY_PARTITION scope, mode, dimension, and factor
rules.summary: ARRAY_PARTITION requests block, cyclic, or complete partitioning to expose parallel accesses, with a resource tradeoff that must be confirmed by tool evidence.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Bind INTERFACE to a concrete port
rules.summary: INTERFACE selects a protocol for a named port; it is a scoped contract request, not proof of system connectivity.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Treat STREAM depth as a scoped channel request
rules.summary: STREAM requests channel implementation details such as depth for a variable; reported FIFO structure and dynamic occupancy remain separate evidence.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Do not turn DEPENDENCE into a proved dependence fact
rules.summary: DEPENDENCE supplies user information for an enclosing loop or function; its optional variable or class selector must not be represented as compiler-proved dependence evidence without independent proof.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: The declared-constraint authority and the prohibition on promotion to a proved static fact are HLSGraph truth-boundary policies.

rules.title: Keep LOOP_TRIPCOUNT separate from proved bounds
rules.summary: LOOP_TRIPCOUNT supplies bounds for reporting and estimation; it is not a proof of the source loop's runtime bound.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Treat INLINE as a hierarchy-transform request
rules.summary: INLINE can remove a function boundary and expose operations to surrounding optimization; achieved hierarchy must come from compiler or report evidence.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Bind C simulation correctness to its testbench
rules.summary: A C simulation pass or failure is evidence for the executed testbench and inputs, not a universal correctness proof.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Bind RTL cosimulation results to their testbench
rules.summary: RTL cosimulation status, latency, interval, mismatch, and deadlock evidence describe the executed testbench and inputs.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Scope dataflow profiling results to the cosimulation workload
rules.summary: FIFO occupancy and stall measurements from RTL cosimulation describe that workload, not every possible input.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Treat schedule and binding as compiler decisions
rules.summary: Control steps, pipeline stages, limiting factors, and operation-to-resource bindings are compiler evidence and should retain source mappings.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Keep latency, interval, and initiation interval distinct
rules.summary: Function latency, transaction interval, loop iteration latency, and loop initiation interval are separate reported measures and must not be collapsed.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Record XDC constraints as versioned design inputs
rules.summary: Implementation results depend on the XDC constraints applied at the relevant flow stage; preserve the constraint artifact with those results.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Hashing the constraint artifact into snapshot identity is an HLSGraph provenance policy.

rules.title: Preserve WNS and TNS as separate stage-scoped observations
rules.summary: WNS and TNS are different timing-summary measures; preserve both values and the stage of the report that produced them.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Keep routed timing observations scoped to their report and stage
rules.summary: A routed timing report and its critical-path observations retain their routed-design, report, and stage identity; their presence alone does not decide a timing gate.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Use routed timing evidence for the post-route timing gate
rules.summary: Timing signoff is checked on a fully placed and routed design, and bus skew is checked separately from the timing summary.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: No executable post-route timing gate is published until routed-design, constraints, timing-summary, and separate bus-skew evidence can be closed together.

rules.title: Record congestion only with placed or routed context
rules.summary: Congestion reporting depends on placement or routing state, so it must retain its implementation stage and report scope.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

rules.title: Preserve utilization report stage and scope
rules.summary: Utilization values are tied to the selected design stage and report scope, which must remain attached to each observation.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

metadata.hlsgraph_local_derivation_policy: Resource fit is a separate HLSGraph derivation over stage-scoped utilization and matching target-capacity evidence; UG906 does not impose a post-route-only resource gate.

rules.section: Running Power Analysis from the Vivado IDE
rules.title: Bind power observations to their activity assumptions
rules.summary: Power analysis combines design state with simulation, manual, or vectorless activity; dynamic and static power values must retain that activity source.
rules.license_note: Short project-authored paraphrase; no vendor text is redistributed.

entries.rationale: The cited section inventories C-synthesis outputs but does not establish that every parsed value is an estimate or define its relationship to implementation-stage observations; no executable rule is published.
The public adapter does not preserve a self-validating waiver-set identity; this section remains a locator, not executable coverage.

entries.section: Running Power Analysis from the Vivado IDE

target_inventory.rationale: The public directive contract cannot yet represent the legal mutually exclusive optional variable and class selectors, so this user assertion remains non-executable.
The container artifact alone does not close one parsed observation to a workload, immutable run manifest, declared output, and live report bytes; query its emitted predicate or correctness gate.
The container artifact alone does not close one parsed observation to a workload, immutable run manifest, declared output, and live report bytes; query its emitted predicate or correctness gate.
The container artifact alone does not close one parsed observation to a workload, immutable run manifest, declared output, and live report bytes; query its emitted predicate or correctness gate.
The cited output section identifies the container but does not establish the estimate-versus-implementation semantics needed for an executable binding.
The container artifact alone does not close one dynamic channel observation to a workload, immutable run manifest, declared output, and live report bytes; query its emitted profile predicate.
The container artifact alone does not identify one directive instance or resolved scope; its emitted directive predicates bind only with exact instance-and-scope evidence.
The normalized container can carry congestion, timing, DRC/CDC, or power fields; the artifact kind alone does not satisfy any one field-specific rule condition.
The normalized container can carry congestion, timing, DRC/CDC, or power fields; the artifact kind alone does not satisfy any one field-specific rule condition.
The checkpoint is a cold routed-design identity; no executable signoff binding is published until timing-summary, constraint, and separate bus-skew evidence are closed together.
Resource fit is an HLSGraph local derivation over stage-scoped utilization and matching target capacities; UG906 does not define a post-route-only gate contract.
UG906 requires a routed design and a separate bus-skew check; the current public gate evidence does not close the complete signoff contract.
Parser failure classification is an HLSGraph infrastructure contract, not a vendor design rule.
Parser failure classification is an HLSGraph infrastructure contract, not a vendor design rule.
Missing-workload detection is an HLSGraph evidence-integrity policy; the diagnostic necessarily lacks the workload identity required for a workload-scoped vendor rule binding.
Fail-closed cross-layer mapping is an HLSGraph evidence policy rather than a vendor specification rule.
Fail-closed cross-layer mapping is an HLSGraph evidence policy rather than a vendor specification rule.
This HLSGraph diagnostic records that the utilization/capacity evidence needed to apply the vendor rule is absent; absence cannot satisfy that rule's utilization-report condition.
This HLSGraph diagnostic records an incomplete evidence closure; it must not activate a vendor rule whose utilization-report condition is not completely satisfied.
The requested period is a declared constraint; the cited C-synthesis output section does not normatively define the extractor predicate.
The cited C-synthesis output section does not establish estimate semantics for this extractor predicate.
The cited performance-metric section does not by itself define the extractor-specific trip-count predicate.
Requested II is represented by the scoped PIPELINE declaration; this report predicate has no independently supported binding in the cited performance-metric section.
Achieved II requires a qualified schedule/report observation; the current cited sections do not close that extractor-specific binding.
The cited performance-metric section does not by itself define the extractor-specific pipeline-depth predicate.
The congestion-analysis rule does not normatively define the independent SLR-crossing count predicate.
The current normalized physical-summary adapter does not preserve a self-validating waiver-set identity, so UG906 waiver guidance cannot be safely bound to this count.
The current normalized physical-summary adapter does not preserve a self-validating waiver-set identity, so UG906 waiver guidance cannot be safely bound to this count.
Available capacity is target/device identity, not a C-synthesis estimate.
Available capacity is target/device identity, not a C-synthesis estimate.
